channel.title: TeslaBlog.eu — Tesla News & Updates
channel.description: Latest Tesla news, deals, and updates across Europe and beyond.

item.title: Elon Musk Just Announced Fanastic News to Nvidia Stock Investors
item.description: These longtime market-beaters still have quite aggressive ambitions.

item.title: Tesla Semi gets new product launch as mass manufacturing hits Plaid Mode
item.description: The Tesla Semi is getting a new production launch as mass manufacturing on the all-electric truck is gearing up to hit Plaid Mode. Tesla has introduced a game-changing addition to its commercial charging lineup with the new 125 kW Basecharger for Semi . Launched this week as part of the new “Semi...

item.title: Elon Musk Testifies He 'Didn't Read' OpenAI's For-Profit Fine Print, Claims Sam Altman Misled Him: 'Can't Steal A Charity'
item.description: On Thursday, Elon Musk told a California courtroom that he backed OpenAI with millions of dollars under the belief it would remain a nonprofit focused on humanity. Musk Says OpenAI Abandoned Its Original Nonprofit Mission During cross-examination, Musk testified that while he was aware of prelimi...

item.title: Amazon and Tesla Drive One-Third of FDIS as U.S. Spending Surges Despite Recession-Level Sentiment
item.description: Consumer discretionary names live or die on whether households feel comfortable opening their wallets, and right now those signals are flashing in opposite directions. Right now those signals are crossing in unusual directions: the University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment Index sits at 53.3 in M...

item.title: 1966 Ford Mustang converted into a Tesla with working ‘Full Self-Driving’
item.description: A Tesla auto parts shop owner in Sacramento spent about $40,000 and two years converting a 1966 Ford Mustang into a fully functional Tesla — complete with the Model 3’s dual-motor drivetrain, 15-inch touchscreen, and working “Full Self-Driving” (Supervised). It’s likely the first non-Tesla vehicl...

item.title: This Tesla owner won $10k in court for Tesla’s FSD lies. Tesla is still fighting him.
item.description: For over a decade now, Tesla has sold a promise of vehicles that can drive themselves, even stating that every car it produced had all the hardware for self-driving . But after years of the company being unable to deliver, some owners want their money back. Ben Gawiser is one of those owners, who...

item.title: Tesla revises new Intervention Reporting system with Full Self-Driving
item.description: Tesla has revised its new Intervention Reporting system within the Full Self-Driving suite that now categorizes reasons that drivers take over when the semi-autonomous driving functionality is active. It is the second revision to the program as Tesla is trying to make it easier to decipher driver...

item.title: Tesla's (NASDAQ:TSLA) Shareholders Have More To Worry About Than Only Soft Earnings
item.description: A lackluster earnings announcement from Tesla, Inc. ( NASDAQ:TSLA ) last week didn't sink the stock price. We think...

item.title: Here’s What Jim Cramer Thinks You Should Do With Tesla (TSLA) Shares
item.description: We have recently shared Jim Cramer Made A Big Prediction About OpenAI & Discussed These 20 Stocks. Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) is one of the stocks discussed by Jim Cramer.

item.title: Who Is Tesla Selling 1 Million Humanoid Robots A Year To?
item.description: In its latest quarterly report for shareholders, Tesla mentions “robots” three times. Two of those times were in the “Robotics” paragraph/section: “Preparations for our first large-scale Optimus factory will begin shortly in Q2. The first-generation line, designed for 1 million robots a year, wil...

item.title: Tesla Got $573 Million from SpaceX and xAI in 2025
item.description: There’s been a lot of talk lately that Tesla may well end up merging with SpaceX, and with both companies shifting more and more toward AI and its supportive infrastructure, perhaps that is now Elon Musk’s big plan. Of course, both Tesla and SpaceX have built their fortunes on other ... [contin...

item.title: Tesla launches Model 3 RWD in Canada at record-low $39,490 ($29,000 USD) from China
item.description: Tesla has launched a new Model 3 Premium RWD trim in Canada starting at just $39,490 CAD — a record-low price for the electric sedan in the country. The price, which converts to roughly $29,000 USD, is made possible by sourcing the vehicle from Giga Shanghai instead of Fremont, California. The mo...

item.title: Robots Everywhere: The First Look At Tesla's Giant Semi Factory
item.description: submitted by /u/twinbee [link] [comments]

item.title: First Semi off high volume line
item.description: Source: https://x.com/tesla_semi/status/2049590842302857675 submitted by /u/twinbee [link] [comments]

item.title: Tesla ends week nearly 4% higher. What's driving the EV stock?
item.description: Tesla shares (TSLA) ended this trading week nearly 4% higher — gaining back some ground from post-earnings declines — after the EV maker announced that the first of its long-awaited Tesla Semi truck has come off the "high volume" production line Yahoo Finance Senior Autos Reporter Pras Subramania...

item.title: What's Going On With Tesla Stock Friday
item.description: Tesla Stock Climbs as Semi Trucks Roll Off Line. Europe Helps

item.title: Tesla generates over $500 Million from Musk-linked companies
item.description: xAI and SpaceX drive growing internal revenue

item.title: Tesla, Rivian, or Lucid: Which EV Stock Came Out Ahead in April?
item.description: The April scoreboard for electric vehicle (EV) stocks delivers a surprise. Rivian (NASDAQ:RIVN) stock outpaced the field with a 9% monthly rally in April, while Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) stock managed a more modest 3% gain. Meanwhile, Lucid Group (NASDAQ:LCID) stock dropped 33%, losing roughly a third ...

item.title: Podcast: Tesla Semi is here, Xpeng VLA 2.0 test drive, Rivian earnings, and more
item.description: In the Electrek Podcast , we discuss the most popular news in the world of sustainable transport and energy. In this week’s episode, we discuss Tesla Semi entering production, our Xpeng VLA 2.0 test drive, Rivian’s earnings, and more. more…

item.title: Tesla Just Dropped Big News About Its Electric Semi Truck
item.description: Tesla Inc is trending after the market close on Wednesday after the company provided an update on the long-awaited Tesla Semi. Tesla High-Volume Semi Production Begins In a social post on X late Wednesday, Tesla announced that its first Tesla Semi has rolled off the high-volume production line. D...

item.title: The Reason GM Stock Is Beating Ford and Tesla. (It Has Nothing to Do With Cars or Tariffs.)
item.description: General Motors was the exception. Tesla reported better pricing and more subscriptions for its Full Self-Driving driver-assistance product. Stellantis was “steady,” if not stellar, according to Oxcap analyst Stuart Pearson.

item.title: Elon Musk Sides With Viral 'Pokémon Economy' Analogy On Free Markets— 'This Is How...'
item.description: Elon Musk reiterated his view on wealth and resource allocation, backing a viral post by an X user. X user, Brivael, on Wednesday, said that the Tesla Inc. CEO’s words about resource allocation “really stuck” with him.

item.title: Tesla launches Basecharger for Semi, reveals $188,000 Megacharger
item.description: Tesla has launched a new “Semi Charging for Business” program that allows fleet operators and businesses to purchase and install their own Megacharger and a brand-new product called the Basecharger — a lower-power 125 kW charger designed for depot and overnight use. The online configurator reveal...

item.title: Tesla stock higher for week as Semi starts production, company nets $573M in sales from SpaceX, xAI last year
item.description: Tesla stock is poised to finish the day and week higher, following last week's drop in the wake of earnings.

item.title: Tesla Sold SpaceX $143 Million Worth Of Cars: SEC Filing
item.description: An updated SEC filing disclosed Tesla's sale of electric vehicles to CEO Elon Musk's other company SpaceX. Tesla stock is up about 1%.

item.title: Tesla reports $158 billion Elon Musk compensation for 2025
item.description: The figure is almost entirely an accounting-driven valuation; no shares have vested and Tesla says Musk's realized pay was zero

item.title: Tesla Is Burning Billions to Build Its AI Future—and the Stock Is Feeling It
item.description: Tesla stock rose in April. The move comes after Tesla stock gained 2.7% in April, its first positive month for 2026. Investors have lofty expectations for Tesla’s plans to populate the world with “physical AI” applications such as robo-taxis and robots.

item.title: Tesla Rivals BYD, Geely, Xiaomi See Sales Bump. These China EV Makers Decline.
item.description: BYD, Geely, Leap and Xiaomi deliveries rose in April as the industry's new models still roll out. Li Auto and Nio saw sales decline.

item.title: Tesla’s former HR chief: the AI layoff panic Is built on a false premise—here’s what most workers need to know
item.description: The hyperscaler math driving cuts at Meta and Microsoft does not apply to the companies where most Americans actually work.

item.title: Tesla sales rebound continues in Europe as rising fuel costs drive EV demand
item.description: Investing.com -- Tesla registrations continued to recover across some European markets in April, with sales rising in Denmark, France and the Netherlands as surging fuel prices following the Iran war drive consumer interest in electric vehicles.

item.title: Tesla Revenue From Musk-Linked Companies Tops $500M
item.description: xAI accounted for most of the related-party revenue last year

item.title: Tesla Reports Musk's $158 Billion Compensation Figure
item.description: Tesla disclosed Musk's 2025 compensation figure, while warning reported pay may differ sharply from realized value.

item.title: Market Chatter: Tesla CEO Elon Musk's SpaceX Starship Development Costs Exceed $15 Billion
item.description: Tesla Chief Executive Elon Musk's SpaceX has spent more than $15 billion developing its next-generat

item.title: Tesla (TSLA) reveals $573M web of transactions between Elon Musk’s companies
item.description: Tesla’s amended annual filing reveals the full scope of the financial web connecting Elon Musk’s companies — $573 million in revenue from SpaceX and xAI alone, plus millions more in expenses flowing to X, The Boring Company, and Musk’s personal security firm. The 10-K/A filing, submitted to the S...

item.title: Tesla sales rebound continues in several European markets in April
item.description: Registrations of Tesla cars continued to rebound in France, Denmark and the Netherlands ‌in April, but fast-moving Chinese rivals such as BYD kept ‌chipping away at the U.S. electric vehicle maker's market share. Tesla's sales have recovered strongly ​in Europe this year after two consecutive ann...

item.title: California hits Tesla Cybercab and Robotaxi driverless cars with new law
item.description: California DMV formally adopted new rules on April 29, 2026 that allow law enforcement to issue “notices of noncompliance”, or in other words, ticket autonomous vehicle companies when their cars commit moving violations. The rules take effect July 1, 2026, officially closes a regulatory gap that ...

item.title: Bitcoin, ETH, XRP, DOGE, MSTR Cashtags Go Live on X Web, Musk Says Most Crypto Are Scams
item.description: Elon Musk’s X rolls out the Smart Cashtags feature for stocks and crypto assets on the web version today. This new feature adds interactive, real-time financial data to users’ timelines, supporting major crypto and stocks trading such as Bitcoin, ETH, XRP, SOL, DOGE, TSLA, MSTR, and COIN. Meanwhi...

item.title: Tesla Made $573 Million in Sales From SpaceX and xAI Last Year
item.description: (Bloomberg) -- Tesla Inc. generated more than half a billion dollars in revenue last year from selling products to two of Elon Musk’s other companies, the carmaker disclosed in an amended annual filing.Most Read from BloombergUS Seeks to Deploy Hypersonic Missile for the First Time Against IranTw...

item.title: Elon Musk’s Tesla Compensation Totals $158 Billion for 2025
item.description: (Bloomberg) -- Elon Musk’s first compensation figure after Tesla Inc. shareholders approved his moonshot pay package is in, and it’s a doozy: $158 billion.Most Read from BloombergUS Seeks to Deploy Hypersonic Missile for the First Time Against IranTwo NJ Malls Separated by Just Four Miles — and V...

item.title: Elon Musk reportedly labels most cryptocurrencies as 'scams'
item.description: Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) CEO Elon Musk has always been championed as being pro-crypto. But a recent reported comment in a court hearing is leaving the crowd puzzled. Musk, who co-founded OpenAI in 2015, is suing the company for allegedly breaching its founding mission of being non-profit after it ...

item.title: Tesla Stock Is Outrageously Cheap. Here's Why There Could Be Another 100% in Upside Potential.
item.description: One opportunity could add $1 trillion to Tesla's market cap.

item.title: Why Is Hertz Stock Up Today? A New Deal With Uber Focuses on Robo-Taxi Fleets.
item.description: Hertz shares are rising following the announcement that it will be part of the maintenance operations for Uber’s robo-taxi offerings.

item.title: Elon Musk Says Most Crypto Are Scams, But X Launches New Crypto Trading Terminal
item.description: Elon Musk called most crypto coins scams during OpenAI trial as X simultaneously launched its Cashtags trading feed.

item.title: Why Tesla Stock Is Climbing Today
item.description: Tesla Stock Rises. Progress on Semi Trucks Lifts Sentiment

item.title: Tesla Model X shocks everyone by crushing every other used car in America
item.description: The Tesla Model X was the fastest-selling used vehicle in the United States in the first quarter of the year, crushing every other used car in America. iSeeCars data for the first quarter shows that the Model X was the fastest-selling used car, lasting just 25.6 days on the market on average, two...

item.title: Tesla Begins Mass Producing Semi Trucks. Stock Rises.
item.description: Tesla's dedicated high-volume production line at its Sparks, Nev., facility successfully manufactured its first Semi trucks. Wednesday's announcement, made via an X post, caps a nearly decade-long wait for mass production of Tesla's commercial trucks, which were first shown the public in 2017.

item.title: The Dawn Project Report to Congress: Tesla's Self-Driving Software Has Been Involved in 59 Deaths, Urgent Regulatory Reform Needed
item.description: Public safety group delivers report documenting Tesla Full Self-Driving failures and calls for three specific regulatory reformsSANTA BARBARA, Calif., April 30, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- The Dawn Project today delivered a report to members of Congress and the National Highway Traffic Safety Admini...

item.title: How SoftBank Can Give Tesla Stock a Boost
item.description: Wednesday evening, the Financial Times reported that SoftBank was going to list an AI robotics company in the U.S., valued at up to $100 billion. The company, called Roze, likely includes assets, including ABB’s robotics division, that SoftBank agreed to buy in October for $5.4 billion. Inves...

item.title: Musk Says No Written Agreement for His Early Donation to OpenAI
item.description: (Bloomberg) -- Elon Musk acknowledged there was no written agreement or contract with OpenAI regarding the terms of his donation to the company when it was first founded as a nonprofit research organization more than a decade ago. Most Read from BloombergUS Seeks to Deploy Hypersonic Missile for ...

item.title: Tesla Hits a Milestone With Semi Truck Production. The Stock Is Up.
item.description: Tesla stock hasn’t gotten a boost from higher oil prices. The move came after Tesla reached a milestone: Its first semi truck rolled off the company’s high-volume production line on Wednesday. The total volume of semi trucks sold in the U.S and Europe each year is closer to 500,000.
